reviewer: A — Peer Subject-Matter Reviewer (Biology PhD / AP reader)

issues.description: Section I (60 MCQ / 90 min / 50%) and Section II (6 FRQ / 90 min / 50%; 2 long FRQs 8–10 pts each + 4 short FRQs 4 pts each) match the official AP Biology exam structure exactly. Total 3 hours, 50/50 weighting is correct.

1–5 scale stated correctly; '3 or higher is a qualifying score earning credit at many institutions' is accurate, and the caveat that each college sets its own policy (some require 4 or 5) is appropriately included.

The six AP Biology science practices are correctly enumerated and glossed: (1) concept explanation, (2) visual representation, (3) question/method (experimental design), (4) data representation, (5) statistical tests (chi-square, error bars), (6) argument from evidence.

Correlation vs causation is stated correctly: correlation = co-variation, causation requires a manipulated variable plus control, lurking/confounding variable (summer heat) drives both. FRQ-safe 'associated with' framing is exactly right.

Exponential (J-curve, dN/dt = rN, unlimited resources) vs logistic (S-curve, dN/dt = rN[(K−N)/K], slows near K) recapped correctly. The nuance that K is not a hard ceiling (overshoot/oscillation) and the density-dependent vs density-independent distinction are accurate.

10% rule (~10% transferred up, ~90% lost as heat; energy FLOWS, matter CYCLES; 10,000→1,000→100 kcal) is correct. Hardy-Weinberg as a null model with all five assumptions (no mutation, no gene flow, no drift/large population, random mating, no selection) and p^2+2pq+q^2=1 is correct. Evolution basics (fitness = reproductive success, selection acts on PHENOTYPES, drift random/non-adaptive, homologous = common ancestor vs analogous = convergent) are all stated correctly.

overall_assessment: Clean. Every exam-format number, timing, and weighting matches the official AP Biology exam structure (Section I: 60 MCQ/90 min/50%; Section II: 6 FRQ/90 min/50% = 2 long 8–10 pts + 4 short 4 pts; 1–5 scoring; 3+ qualifying). All recapped cross-unit concepts — Hardy-Weinberg null model and its five assumptions, exponential vs logistic growth, the 10% rule with energy-flows/matter-cycles, biomagnification framing, correlation vs causation, and natural selection acting on phenotypes — are stated accurately and at AP-appropriate precision. No factual errors found. This synthesis module is exam-accurate and safe to publish.
